sections.title: PRODUCT DEFINITION
sections.text:
Canned Salmon is the product prepared from headed and eviscerated fish of any of the species listed below from which the fins and tails have been removed, and to which salt, water, salmon oil and/or other edible oils may have been added.
- Salmo salar
- Oncorhynchus nerka
- Oncorhynchus kisutch
- Oncorhynchus tschawytscha
- Oncorhynchus gorbuscha
- Oncorhynchus keta
- Oncorhynchus masou

sections.title: PROCESS DEFINITION
sections.text: Canned salmon is packed in hermetically sealed containers and shall have received a processing treatment sufficient to ensure commercial sterility.

sections.title: SALMON
sections.text: The product shall be prepared from sound fish of the species in Section 2.1 and of a quality fit to be sold fresh for human consumption.

sections.title: Canned salmon shall consist of sections which are cut transversely from the fish and which are filled vertically into the can.  The sections shall be packed so that the cut surfaces are approximately parallel with the ends of the container.

sections.title: SAMPLING
sections.text: (i) Sampling of lots for examination of the final product as prescribed in Section 3.3 shall be in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5. (ii) Sampling of lots for examination of net weight shall be carried out in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan meeting the criteria established by the CAC.

sections.title: SENSORY EVALUATION AND PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
sections.text: Samples taken for sensory and physical examination shall be assessed by persons trained in such examination and in accordance with Section 7.3, Annex A and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CXG 31-1999).

sections.title: DETERMINATION OF NET WEIGHT
sections.text:
Net contents of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedure:
(i) Weigh the unopened container.
(ii) Open the container and remove the contents.
(iii) Weigh the empty container, (including the end) after removing excess liquid and adhering meat.
(iv) Subtract the weight of the empty container from the weight of the unopened container. The resultant figure will be the net content.

page_spans.text:
Net contents of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedure:
(i) Weigh the unopened container.
(ii) Open the container and remove the contents.
(iii) Weigh the empty container, (including the end) after removing excess liquid and adhering meat.
(iv) Subtract the weight of the empty container from the weight of the unopened container. The resultant figure will be the net content.

sections.title: DETERMINATION OF DRAINED WEIGHT FOR PRODUCTS PACKED WITH EDIBLE OILS OTHER THAN SALMON OIL
path_titles: DETERMINATION OF DRAINED WEIGHT FOR PRODUCTS PACKED WITH EDIBLE OILS OTHER THAN SALMON OIL
sections.text:
The drained weight of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedure:
(i) Maintain the container at a temperature between 20 °C and 30 °C for a minimum of 12 hours prior to examination.
(ii) Open and tilt the container to distribute the contents on a pre-weighed circular sieve which consists of wire mesh with square openings of 2.8 mm x 2.8 mm.
(iii) Incline the sieve at an angle of approximately 17 -20° and allow the fish to drain for two minutes, measured from the time the product is poured into the sieve.
(iv) Weigh the sieve containing the drained fish.
(v) The weight of drained fish is obtained by subtracting the weight of the sieve from the weight of the sieve and drained product.
sections.retrieval_text:

sections.title: DEFINITION OF DEFECTIVES
sections.text: A sample unit will be considered defective when it exhibits any of the properties defined below.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF DEFECTIVES
number=8
level=1
A sample unit will be considered defective when it exhibits any of the properties defined below.

page_spans.text: A sample unit will be considered defective when it exhibits any of the properties defined below.

sections.title: FOREIGN MATTER
sections.text: The presence in the sample unit of any matter, which has not been derived from salmon or the packing medium does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determined by any method including magnification that indicates non-compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF DEFECTIVES > FOREIGN MATTER
number=8.1
level=2
The presence in the sample unit of any matter, which has not been derived from salmon or the packing medium does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determined by any method including magnification that indicates non-compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.

sections.title: TEXTURE
sections.text:
(i) Excessive mushy flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation; or
(ii) Excessively tough flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation; or
(iii) Honey combed flesh in excess of 5% of the net contents.

sections.title: LOT ACCEPTANCE
SENSORY AND PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
sections.text:
1. Complete external can examination for the presence of container integrity defects or can ends which may be distorted outward.
2. Open can and complete weight determination according to defined procedures in Section 7.3 and 7.4.
3. Examine product for discolouration, foreign and objectionable matter. The presence of hard bone is an indicator of underprocessing and will require an evaluation for sterility.
4. Assess odour, flavour and texture in accordance with the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CXG 31-1999)
sections.retrieval_text:
SENSORY AND PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
number=ANNEX "A"
level=1
1. Complete external can examination for the presence of container integrity defects or can ends which may be distorted outward.
2. Open can and complete weight determination according to defined procedures in Section 7.3 and 7.4.
3. Examine product for discolouration, foreign and objectionable matter. The presence of hard bone is an indicator of underprocessing and will require an evaluation for sterility.
4. Assess odour, flavour and texture in accordance with the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CXG 31-1999)

page_spans.text:
1. Complete external can examination for the presence of container integrity defects or can ends which may be distorted outward.
2. Open can and complete weight determination according to defined procedures in Section 7.3 and 7.4.
3. Examine product for discolouration, foreign and objectionable matter. The presence of hard bone is an indicator of underprocessing and will require an evaluation for sterility.
4. Assess odour, flavour and texture in accordance with the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CXG 31-1999)

sentences.original:
Net contents of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedure:
(i) Weigh the unopened container.
(ii) Open the container and remove the contents.
(iii) Weigh the empty container, (including the end) after removing excess liquid and adhering meat.
(iv) Subtract the weight of the empty container from the weight of the unopened container.

prefilter.reason: Describes a calculation method, relevant for procedures.

classification.sentence: The resultant figure will be the net content.
classification.function: Define term resulting from procedure
classification.pattern: Simple definitional declarative
classification.justification: Simple definition statement outside obligation or condition modalities; no template match.

classification.function: Define term resulting from procedure
classification.pattern: Simple definitional declarative
classification.justification: Simple definition statement outside obligation or condition modalities; no template match.

sentences.original:
The drained weight of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedure:
(i) Maintain the container at a temperature between 20 °C and 30 °C for a minimum of 12 hours prior to examination.

classification.sentence: (v) The weight of drained fish is obtained by subtracting the weight of the sieve from the weight of the sieve and drained product.
classification.function: Define calculation for weight of drained fish
classification.pattern: Declarative defining calculation method

components.Calculation: weight of drained fish equals weight of sieve and drained product minus weight of sieve

classification.justification: Declarative statement defining calculation formula; no obligation or condition; novel pattern.

sentences.original: A sample unit will be considered defective when it exhibits any of the properties defined below.

prefilter.reason: Introduction to a property list, relevant for quality assessment.

classification.sentence: A sample unit will be considered defective when it exhibits any of the properties defined below.
classification.function: Define condition for defectiveness based on properties
classification.pattern: Conditional declarative with future passive modality

components.Subject: A sample unit
components.Condition: when it exhibits any properties defined below

classification.justification: Conditional classification of defectiveness; fits condition modality with future passive phrasing; partial template match.

sentences.original: The presence in the sample unit of any matter, which has not been derived from salmon or the packing medium does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determined by any method including magnification that indicates non-compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.

prefilter.reason: Describes non-compliance with manufacturing practices, relevant for regulatory filtering.

classification.sentence: The presence in the sample unit of any matter, which has not been derived from salmon or the packing medium does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determined by any method including magnification that indicates non-compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.
classification.function: Definitional condition clarifying when a presence does not pose a threat or indicates non-compliance
classification.pattern: Complex conditional statement with multiple qualifiers and exception clauses explaining presence and recognition criteria

components.condition: presence in the sample unit of any matter not derived from salmon or packing medium
components.effect: does not pose a threat to human health
components.recognition: readily recognized without magnification or present at level determined by any method including magnification indicating non-compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices

classification.justification: Complex conditional definition with negative phrasing and recognition thresholds does not match existing templates; modality is condition due to qualification of presence impact.
notes: Sentence combines health risk condition and recognition criteria with exception clauses.

sentences.original: A sample unit affected by persistent and distinct objectionable odours or flavours indicative of decomposition or rancidity.

prefilter.reason: Indicates product issues that are regulated.

classification.sentence: A sample unit affected by persistent and distinct objectionable odours or flavours indicative of decomposition or rancidity.
classification.function: Identification of defect condition in sample unit relating to odours or flavours
classification.pattern: Noun phrase describing affected sample unit with qualifier indicating defect based on sensory indicators

components.sample_unit_condition: affected by persistent and distinct objectionable odours or flavours indicative of decomposition or rancidity

classification.justification: Declarative noun phrase identifying sample unit defect condition; no prescriptive modality; lacks match to templates; modality assigned as Condition due to indication of defect as condition.
notes: Simple defect description resembles condition but lacks explicit obligation or limit.

sentences.original:
(i) Excessive mushy flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation; or
(ii) Excessively tough flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation; or
(iii) Honey combed flesh in excess of 5% of the net contents.

classification.sentence:
(i) Excessive mushy flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation; or
(ii) Excessively tough flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation; or
(iii) Honey combed flesh in excess of 5% of the net contents.
classification.function: Enumeration of defect conditions for sample units based on flesh texture and extent
classification.pattern: List of enumerated defect conditions with qualitative and quantitative qualifiers

components.defect_1: Excessive mushy flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation
components.defect_2: Excessively tough flesh uncharacteristic of the species in the presentation
components.defect_3: Honey combed flesh in excess of 5% of the net contents

classification.justification: Enumerated list of defect conditions with qualitative and measured criteria; no direct obligation or prohibition; modality assigned as Condition for identification of triggers.
notes: Lists defects with presence thresholds, typical in condition clauses but no explicit normative modality.

sentences.original: A sample unit affected by distinct discolouration indicative of decomposition or rancidity or by sulphide staining of the meat exceeding 5% of the net contents.

prefilter.reason: Features that may trigger regulatory concern.

classification.sentence: A sample unit affected by distinct discolouration indicative of decomposition or rancidity or by sulphide staining of the meat exceeding 5% of the net contents.
classification.function: Statement identifying defect condition in sample unit by discolouration or staining presence and extent
classification.pattern: Noun phrase naming affected sample unit with defect qualifiers including quantitative limit

components.sample_unit_condition: affected by distinct discolouration indicative of decomposition or rancidity
components.additional_condition: sulphide staining of the meat exceeding 5% of the net contents

classification.justification: Simple description of defect conditions with specified extent; modality is condition describing defect presence and threshold; no match to normative templates.
notes: Describes defect presence and quantification but no prescriptive statement.

sentences.original: A sample unit affected by struvite crystals - any struvite crystal greater than 5 mm in length.

prefilter.reason: Describes a condition of potential regulatory relevance.

classification.sentence: A sample unit affected by struvite crystals - any struvite crystal greater than 5 mm in length.
classification.function: Identification of physical defect condition based on presence and size of crystals in sample unit
classification.pattern: Noun phrase with appositive specifying defect characteristic with quantitative threshold

components.size_threshold: any struvite crystal greater than 5 mm in length

classification.justification: Describes defect and size threshold without prescriptive normative modality; modality assigned as Condition describing defect criteria; no known template match.
notes: Simple defect condition with a size limit but stated as condition, not obligation or limit.

classification.function: Conditional statement specifying criteria for lot acceptance against standard requirements
classification.pattern: Main clause with obligation modal 'shall' followed by multiple enumerated conditional criteria for compliance evaluation

components.subject: A lot
components.obligation: shall be considered as meeting the requirements of this standard
conditions: total number of defectives does not exceed acceptance number with AQL 6.5
total number of sample units not meeting form of presentation does not exceed acceptance number with AQL 6.5
average net and drained weights not less than declared with no unreasonable shortage
Food Additives, Hygiene and Labelling requirements met

classification.justification: Strong conditional obligation with enumerated quantitative and qualitative conditions; partial match to typical lot acceptance templates but detailed enumeration causes adaptation.
notes: Complex multi-part conditions with normative obligation modality.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CANNED SALMON
pages.summary: Página contém o título do documento e cabeçalho. Três seções principais (1, 2, 3) com subseções e subsubseções bem definidas e numeradas. Texto interior associado corretamente aos nós adequados. A seção 3.1 continua na próxima página.

Página contém seções principais 3.2 a 7 com subtítulos numerados hierarquicamente. Texto interno associado corretamente a cada nó. Não há continuidade explícita de seções da página anterior ou para a próxima página.

Página contém seção 7.3 e 7.4 com procedimentos detalhados para determinação de pesos, seguida da seção 8 com definição de produtos defeituosos e suas subcategorias numeradas. Nenhuma seção indica continuidade da página anterior ou para a próxima página. Os títulos estão claros e o texto está bem associado.

Página contém duas seções principais numeradas 8.5 e 9, cada uma com texto associado claro e completo. Nenhuma seção indica continuidade da página anterior ou para a próxima página. Estrutura hierárquica clara e adequada para RAG.

A página contém um título de nível 1 representando o ANEXO A, seguido por uma lista numerada com quatro pontos detalhando o exame sensorial e físico. Não há indicação de continuidade de outras páginas nem para a próxima página.
